articles.title: FIFA’s Boss Gets a Big Boost for a Fourth Term — Wait, Is That Even Allowed?
articles.summary: Gianni Infantino wants to lead FIFA again, and now Africa and Asia say they’ll vote for him. That’s 101 votes out of 211 — almost half! But some people say his first term shouldn’t count, so he can run one more time.

articles.title: Louisiana’s Big Election Pause: Why Voting Maps Got So Messy
articles.summary: Louisiana’s primary elections are suddenly on hold because the Supreme Court said one voting district was drawn unfairly. Now, with early voting supposed to start Saturday, no one knows when people will vote — and it could change who wins in November.

articles.title: A 500-Mile Road Trip for a Campus Show: Inside Turning Point’s Wild Idaho Finale
articles.summary: Turning Point USA’s college tour had some stops with tiny crowds, but in Idaho, hundreds lined up hours early — and over 1,000 were turned away. Two fans drove from California and Nevada just to see conservative speakers Matt Walsh and Michael Knowles.
